query: Angular OnPush change detection debugging interview question

competitors.label: Angular Skipping Component Subtrees
competitors.selectionReason: This is the official Angular guide for OnPush subtree skipping and trigger behavior, making it the canonical competitor.
competitors.winReason: The Angular docs are authoritative on trigger rules, but our page is more direct about the real production bug, the async-pipe default, and the markForCheck versus detectChanges follow-up that interviewers usually push next.
theirStrengths: It clearly documents when Angular skips or checks an OnPush subtree.
theirGaps: It is less organized around the concrete stale-UI bug that makes OnPush memorable in interviews.

realWorldUseCases: This almost always means you mutated an object or array instead of creating a new reference.
actionableExamples: Second real bug variant: manual subscription updates state, but the view still looks stale
followUpConfusion: Assuming a parent mutating child.input.deep.nestedValue should refresh the child even though the input reference stayed the same

realWorldUseCases: The official guide explains subtree skipping and triggers, but our page centers the common production symptom instead of only the rule set.
actionableExamples: Angular.dev still has clearer trigger diagrams and official examples, so the example comparison stays close.
followUpConfusion: The docs cover why OnPush still updates on some events, but our page more directly explains async pipe, manual CDR, and mutation bugs together.

actionableExamples: Add one smaller detached-view or zoneless example if we want to beat the official guide on total example breadth.

competitors.label: GreatFrontEnd Angular Experienced Interview Questions
competitors.selectionReason: GreatFrontEnd directly covers OnPush, Angular DevTools, and performance debugging, so it is the right interview-prep comparator.
competitors.winReason: GreatFrontEnd is strong on performance profiling, but our page is more focused on the classic stale-UI bug and the exact follow-up mechanics behind OnPush updates.
theirStrengths: It ties OnPush to profiling, DevTools, and large-app performance.
theirGaps: It spreads the discussion across a broader experienced-Angular checklist rather than one focused debugging answer.

realWorldUseCases: This almost always means you mutated an object or array instead of creating a new reference.
actionableExamples: Second real bug variant: manual subscription updates state, but the view still looks stale
Use Angular DevTools or a quick logging pass to verify which component is actually being checked.

realWorldUseCases: The experienced guide connects OnPush to performance and profiling, but our page is more immediately useful for the concrete stale-UI failure mode.
actionableExamples: Its Angular DevTools and profiler advice makes the example comparison close.
followUpConfusion: It is lighter on the precise mutation-versus-reference rule and the markForCheck versus detectChanges distinction.

actionableExamples: Add one explicit zoneless or detached-change-detector case if we want to beat a broad experienced-Angular interview guide on example spread.

competitors.label: Angular University OnPush Change Detection Guide
competitors.selectionReason: This is the strongest long-form tutorial on OnPush pitfalls and real examples, so it is the best tutorial/reference competitor.
competitors.winReason: Angular University still goes deeper as a full OnPush tutorial, but our page now wins the compact production-debug framing: two concrete stale-UI bug variants, a cleaner mutation-versus-reference mental model, and a clearer parent-child boundary explanation.
theirStrengths: It is a dedicated OnPush guide with concrete pitfall examples and more step-by-step depth.
theirGaps: It is longer and less compact than a short interview answer.

realWorldUseCases: Second real bug variant: manual subscription updates state, but the view still looks stale
followUpConfusion: Assuming a parent mutating child.input.deep.nestedValue should refresh the child even though the input reference stayed the same
Use Angular DevTools or a quick logging pass to verify which component is actually being checked.

realWorldUseCases: Angular University is also organized around real OnPush failures, but our page now includes a second stale-UI production variant instead of centering only the classic input-mutation bug.
actionableExamples: Its pitfall walkthroughs and code examples go deeper than our compact answer.
followUpConfusion: It already handles common follow-ups about mutation, observables, and nested component behavior, but our page now answers the parent-child nested-property trap and DevTools-driven validation more directly.
